The user wants a market overview but they also flagged that they want to focus on the regulatory angle, especially the EU AI Act implications. They mentioned earlier that they care less about model benchmarks and more about which providers offer compliance tooling out of the box. Given that the writer agent has the regulatory expertise in this crew and I'm the researcher, I think the right move is to delegate the market overview to the writer agent rather than pull market reports myself.

Two options for fetching the customer's purchase history: query_orders gives raw rows but is slow on large accounts, while customer_summary returns a pre-aggregated view that's faster but loses item-level detail. For this audit task I need item-level detail because the dispute is about a specific SKU. I should use query_orders despite the latency cost — the speed advantage of customer_summary is moot if it can't answer the actual question.

Planning the refactor of the auth module. The deprecated SessionStore class has 14 callers, all in routes/. The new TokenStore replaces it but signatures differ on three methods. To do this safely I'll first add a deprecation warning to SessionStore so any production callers we missed will surface in logs, then migrate routes/auth.py first (it's the smallest), then the others. So my first step right now is adding the deprecation warning.
